edges.description: Undersulfated proteoglycans disrupt growth plate matrix, impairing endochondral bone formation.
SLC26A2 deficiency leads to defective collagen secretion with intracellular retention of type II and IX collagens.
ER accumulation of collagens preferentially activates the ATF6 arm of the UPR in chondrocytes.
ATF6 induces excessive FGFR3 expression, leading to overactivation of FGFR3 signaling in chondrocytes.

nodes.description: The ATF6 branch of the unfolded protein response is preferentially activated in SLC26A2-deficient chondrocytes. ATF6 activation drives transcriptional upregulation of FGFR3, linking ER stress to growth factor signaling dysregulation.

evidence.reference_title: Suppressing UPR-dependent overactivation of FGFR3 signaling ameliorates SLC26A2-deficient chondrodysplasias.
evidence.snippet: As a consequence, the ATF6 arm of the unfolded protein response (UPR) is preferentially triggered to overactivate FGFR3 signaling by inducing excessive FGFR3 in slc26a2-/- chondrocytes.

nodes.description: Shortened digits are part of the characteristic hand phenotype in DTD and can be recognized prenatally.

evidence.reference_title: Diastrophic dysplasia: a specific prenatal diagnosis by ultrasound.
evidence.snippet: shortened phalanges

evidence.reference_title: SLC26A2-Associated Diastrophic Dysplasia and rMED-Clinical Features in Affected Finnish Children and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: Radiological findings in DTD include shortened long bones with metaphyseal flaring, flat epiphyses, kyphoscoliosis, cervical kyphosis, bowed radius and tibia, proximally situated "hitchhiker" thumb with shortness of the first metacarpal, brachydactyly and ulnar deviation of fingers.

nodes.description: Cervical kyphosis is usually apparent at birth or in early infancy. It often resolves during growth, but severe cases may require surgery or cause neurologic compromise.

evidence.reference_title: Cervical kyphosis in diastrophic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: In the 24 patients, the kyphosis resolved spontaneously at an average age of 7.1 years.

evidence.reference_title: SLC26A2-Associated Diastrophic Dysplasia and rMED-Clinical Features in Affected Finnish Children and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: cervical kyphosis was present in 11 subjects (79%).

nodes.description: Cleft palate is a common craniofacial manifestation of DTD.

evidence.reference_title: SLC26A2 disease spectrum in Sweden - high frequency of recessive multiple epiphyseal dysplasia (rMED).
evidence.snippet: Clinical features include short stature, joint contractures, spinal deformities, and cleft palate.

evidence.reference_title: SLC26A2-Associated Diastrophic Dysplasia and rMED-Clinical Features in Affected Finnish Children and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: Cleft palate was present in 64%.

nodes.description: Congenital talipes equinovarus is common and often bilateral.

evidence.reference_title: SLC26A2-Associated Diastrophic Dysplasia and rMED-Clinical Features in Affected Finnish Children and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: Seven subjects had bilateral club foot deformity, one had unilateral club foot and two had metatarsus adductus deformity.

evidence.reference_title: Diastrophic dysplasia: a specific prenatal diagnosis by ultrasound.
evidence.snippet: club feet, ulnar diviation of hands, shortened phalanges, and, in particular, abduction of thumbs ('hitchhiker thumbs') and big toes.

nodes.description: In severe SLC26A2 deficiency, chondrocytes fail to secrete type II and type IX collagen normally. These collagens accumulate in the endoplasmic reticulum, causing ER distension and triggering the unfolded protein response.

evidence.reference_title: Suppressing UPR-dependent overactivation of FGFR3 signaling ameliorates SLC26A2-deficient chondrodysplasias.
evidence.snippet: Unexpectedly, slc26a2-/- chondrocytes are defective for collagen secretion, exhibiting intracellular retention and compromised extracellular deposition of ColII and ColIX.

nodes.description: Auricular swelling is a characteristic cartilage manifestation in DTD. It commonly appears in early infancy and may heal with permanent deformity.

evidence.reference_title: Prevention of auricular deformity in children with diastrophic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: In diastrophic dysplasia, auricular swelling commonly occurs in early infancy, inevitably leading to deformity.

evidence.reference_title: SLC26A2-Associated Diastrophic Dysplasia and rMED-Clinical Features in Affected Finnish Children and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: five had auricular abnormalities (swelling/deformities).

nodes.description: Marked short-limbed short stature is present from birth and growth failure is progressive through childhood.

evidence.reference_title: Growth in diastrophic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: The median adult height was 135.7 cm for the male and 129.0 cm for the female subjects. The growth failure was progressive partly because of absent or weak pubertal growth spurt.

evidence.reference_title: SLC26A2-Associated Diastrophic Dysplasia and rMED-Clinical Features in Affected Finnish Children and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: In our cohort, all children with DTD phenotype had a short stature.

nodes.description: Excessive FGFR3 signaling, driven by ATF6-mediated transcriptional upregulation, inhibits chondrocyte proliferation and disrupts the balance between proliferation and apoptosis in growth plate cartilage. This represents a major pathogenic mechanism distinct from the primary matrix defect and a potential therapeutic target.

biological_processes: Fibroblast Growth Factor Receptor Signaling Pathway

evidence.reference_title: Suppressing UPR-dependent overactivation of FGFR3 signaling ameliorates SLC26A2-deficient chondrodysplasias.
evidence.snippet: Consistently, suppressing FGFR3 signaling by blocking either FGFR3 or phosphorylation of the downstream effector favors the recovery of slc26a2-/- cartilage cultures from impaired growth and unbalanced cell proliferation and apoptosis.

evidence.reference_title: Targeting FGFR3 signaling and drug repurposing for the treatment of SLC26A2-related chondrodysplasia in mouse model.
evidence.snippet: Genetic ablation of Fgfr3 in embryonic Slc26a2-deficient chondrocytes slightly attenuated chondrodysplasia.

nodes.description: Experimental FGFR inhibition (e.g., NVP-BGJ398) has rescued impaired cartilage growth in SLC26A2-deficient mouse models by counteracting UPR-dependent FGFR3 overactivation. Both in vitro cartilage culture and in utero maternal administration showed therapeutic effects.

evidence.reference_title: Suppressing UPR-dependent overactivation of FGFR3 signaling ameliorates SLC26A2-deficient chondrodysplasias.
evidence.snippet: Moreover, administration of an FGFR inhibitor to pregnant females shows therapeutic effects on pathological features in slc26a2-/- newborns.

evidence.reference_title: Targeting FGFR3 signaling and drug repurposing for the treatment of SLC26A2-related chondrodysplasia in mouse model.
evidence.snippet: in vivo experiments showed significant alleviation of impaired chondrocyte differentiation, and micro-CT analysis showed a clear improvement in trabecular bone microarchitectural parameters.

nodes.description: Finger symphalangism is a common component of the characteristic hand phenotype in DTD.

evidence.reference_title: SLC26A2-Associated Diastrophic Dysplasia and rMED-Clinical Features in Affected Finnish Children and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: Hand abnormalities were present in all subjects; the most common findings were symphalangism of the fingers (n = 7), hitchhiker’s thumb/abduction of the thumb (n = 7), flexion tendency of the fingers (n = 6) and lack of proximal interphalangeal joints (n = 1).

nodes.description: Valgus knee deformity is a prominent lower-limb manifestation in DTD.

evidence.reference_title: SLC26A2-Associated Diastrophic Dysplasia and rMED-Clinical Features in Affected Finnish Children and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: The prevalence of knee problems was high; valgus deformity (86%), lateral position of patella (79%) and absence/laxity of the anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) (71%) were the main features.

evidence.reference_title: Total knee arthroplasty in patients with diastrophic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: The knee joint often has an abnormal valgus position and is unstable, and degeneration of all joint compartments occurs, even during growth.

nodes.description: Abduction of the thumb is a characteristic hand finding that can be recognized prenatally.

evidence.reference_title: Diastrophic dysplasia: a specific prenatal diagnosis by ultrasound.
evidence.snippet: and, in particular, abduction of thumbs ('hitchhiker thumbs') and big toes.

evidence.reference_title: SLC26A2-Associated Diastrophic Dysplasia and rMED-Clinical Features in Affected Finnish Children and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: Hand abnormalities were present in all subjects; the most common findings were symphalangism of the fingers (n = 7), hitchhiker’s thumb/abduction of the thumb (n = 7), flexion tendency of the fingers (n = 6) and lack of proximal interphalangeal joints (n = 1).

nodes.description: Defective cartilage matrix quality disrupts growth plate architecture. The columnar arrangement of proliferative chondrocytes and the transition to hypertrophic chondrocytes are disordered, with delayed formation of secondary ossification centers and reduced chondrocyte proliferation, leading to disproportionate skeletal growth.

evidence.reference_title: A diastrophic dysplasia sulfate transporter (SLC26A2) mutant mouse: morphological and biochemical characterization of the resulting chondrodysplasia phenotype.
evidence.snippet: The skeletal phenotype included reduced toluidine blue staining of cartilage, chondrocytes of irregular size, delay in the formation of the secondary ossification center and osteoporosis of long bones.

evidence.reference_title: Identification of potential non-invasive biomarkers in diastrophic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Lower than normal CXM levels were observed in most patients, even if the marker did not show a clear pattern in our small patient cohort because CXM values are highly dependent on age, gender and growth velocity.

evidence.reference_title: Matrix disruptions, growth, and degradation of cartilage with impaired sulfation.
evidence.snippet: Collagen orientation was reduced, and the reduction correlated with chondroitin undersulfation. Such disorientation involved the layer of collagen covering the articular surface and protecting cartilage from degradation.

nodes.description: Progressive contractures of large joints, especially knees, elbows, and hips. Hip flexion contractures become evident in infancy in over 90% of patients and are progressive.

evidence.reference_title: SLC26A2 disease spectrum in Sweden - high frequency of recessive multiple epiphyseal dysplasia (rMED).
evidence.snippet: Clinical features include short stature, joint contractures, spinal deformities, and cleft palate.

evidence.reference_title: Development of the hip in diastrophic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Flexion contracture of the hip became evident later in 93% and was progressive.

nodes.description: Pronounced lumbar lordosis is a common spinal deformity in DTD.

evidence.reference_title: Cervical kyphosis in diastrophic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: spinal deformities such as cervical kyphosis, scoliosis, and exaggerated lumbar lordosis.

evidence.reference_title: SLC26A2-Associated Diastrophic Dysplasia and rMED-Clinical Features in Affected Finnish Children and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: Pronounced lumbar lordosis was present in eight subjects (57%)

nodes.description: Patellar instability and luxation are common components of the DTD knee phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: SLC26A2-Associated Diastrophic Dysplasia and rMED-Clinical Features in Affected Finnish Children and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: Patellar luxation was seen in over half of the cohort.

evidence.reference_title: Total knee arthroplasty in patients with diastrophic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Preoperatively, ten knees had chronic dislocation of the patella.

nodes.description: Degenerative joint disease affects weight-bearing joints early, especially the hips and knees.

evidence.reference_title: Total knee arthroplasty in patients with diastrophic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Diastrophic dysplasia results in severe disproportionate short stature, generalized joint deformities, and early osteoarthritis.

evidence.reference_title: Development of the hip in diastrophic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: The changes in the hip led to secondary osteoarthritis before early middle age.

nodes.description: Respiratory insufficiency can complicate the neonatal period in DTD and may require intensive care.

evidence.reference_title: SLC26A2-Associated Diastrophic Dysplasia and rMED-Clinical Features in Affected Finnish Children and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: Five subjects (36%) had respiratory insufficiency after birth; three of them were treated at the intensive care unit and one had severe pulmonary hypertension.

evidence.reference_title: SLC26A2 disease spectrum in Sweden - high frequency of recessive multiple epiphyseal dysplasia (rMED).
evidence.snippet: Diastrophic dysplasia (DTD) is an autosomal recessive skeletal dysplasia caused by SLC26A2 mutations.

evidence.reference_title: SLC26A2-Associated Diastrophic Dysplasia and rMED-Clinical Features in Affected Finnish Children and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: From the subjects with available genetic data, 75% (9/12) were homozygous for the Finnish founder mutation c.-26+2T>C.

nodes.description: Scoliosis affects a substantial subset of patients and may appear in infancy or later childhood.

evidence.reference_title: SLC26A2-Associated Diastrophic Dysplasia and rMED-Clinical Features in Affected Finnish Children and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: Five children (36%) had scoliosis. The children obtained their diagnosis of scoliosis at 0-14 years of age. One child had a rapidly progressing scoliosis at age 1 year.

nodes.description: A small or short chin is common in DTD; prenatal reports may describe this craniofacial finding as micrognathia.

evidence.reference_title: Diastrophic dysplasia: a specific prenatal diagnosis by ultrasound.
evidence.snippet: micrognathia, cervical kyphosis, persistent extension limitation in elbow and knee joints, club feet

evidence.reference_title: SLC26A2-Associated Diastrophic Dysplasia and rMED-Clinical Features in Affected Finnish Children and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: ten had small chin

nodes.description: Loss of SLC26A2 transporter activity reduces intracellular sulfate in chondrocytes, osteoblasts, and fibroblasts, causing undersulfation of cartilage proteoglycans and altering extracellular matrix composition. Proteoglycans bearing glycosaminoglycan chains that are poorly sulfated but of normal length cannot maintain proper matrix hydration and mechanical properties.

evidence.reference_title: Alteration of proteoglycan sulfation affects bone growth and remodeling.
evidence.snippet: Diastrophic dysplasia (DTD) is a chondrodysplasia caused by mutations in the SLC26A2 gene, leading to reduced intracellular sulfate pool in chondrocytes, osteoblasts and fibroblasts.

evidence.reference_title: Undersulfation of proteoglycans synthesized by chondrocytes from a patient with achondrogenesis type 1B homozygous for an L483P substitution in the diastrophic dysplasia sulfate transporter.
evidence.snippet: The results indicate that the defect of sulfate transport is expressed in both chondrocytes and fibroblasts and results in the synthesis of proteoglycans bearing glycosaminoglycan chains which are poorly sulfated but of normal length.

evidence.reference_title: Matrix disruptions, growth, and degradation of cartilage with impaired sulfation.
evidence.snippet: undersulfation of dtd was mild in most regions, but strong in narrow articular and growth plate regions crucial for bone development. This undersulfation correlated with the chondroitin synthesis rate measured via radioactive sulfate incorporation
